title: County seat

infobox.value: This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/County_seat "Special:EditPage/County seat") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["County seat"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22County+seat%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22County+seat%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22County+seat%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22County+seat%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22County+seat%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22County+seat%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22County+seat%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22County+seat%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22County+seat%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22County+seat%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is an administrative center, [seat of government](/wiki/Seat_of_government "Seat of government"), or capital city of a [county](/wiki/County "County") or [civil parish](/wiki/Parish_(administrative_division) "Parish (administrative division)"). The term is in use in six countries: [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), [China](/wiki/China "China"), [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), and the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). An equivalent term, **shire town**, is used in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont") and in several other English-speaking jurisdictions.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

of its county. Generally, the county legislature, [county courthouse](/wiki/County_courthouse "County courthouse"), [sheriff's department headquarters](/wiki/Sheriffs_in_the_United_States "Sheriffs in the United States"), hall of records, [jail](/wiki/County_Jail "County Jail") and [correctional facility](/wiki/Correctional_facility "Correctional facility") are located in the county seat, though some functions (such as highway maintenance, which usually requires a large garage for vehicles, along with asphalt and salt storage facilities) may also be located or conducted in other parts of the county, especially if it is geographically large.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Administrative center for a county or civil parish

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/County_seat "Special:EditPage/County seat") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["County seat"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22County+seat%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22County+seat%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22County+seat%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22County+seat%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22County+seat%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22County+seat%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

A **county seat** is an administrative center, [seat of government](/wiki/Seat_of_government "Seat of government"), or capital city of a [county](/wiki/County "County") or [civil parish](/wiki/Parish_(administrative_division) "Parish (administrative division)"). The term is in use in six countries: [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), [China](/wiki/China "China"), [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), and the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). An equivalent term, **shire town**, is used in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont") and in several other English-speaking jurisdictions.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Canada")]

In [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), the [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_and_territories_of_Canada "Provinces and territories of Canada") of [Ontario](/wiki/List_of_census_divisions_of_Ontario#Regional_municipalities "List of census divisions of Ontario"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), [New Brunswick](/wiki/New_Brunswick "New Brunswick"), [Prince Edward Island](/wiki/Prince_Edward_Island "Prince Edward Island"), and [Nova Scotia](/wiki/Nova_Scotia "Nova Scotia") have counties as an administrative division of government below the provincial level, and thus county seats. In the provinces of Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, and Nova Scotia, the term "shire town" is used in place of county seat.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## China

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: China")]

Main article: [Counties of China](/wiki/Counties_of_China "Counties of China")

County seats in China are the administrative centers of the counties in the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/China "China").
They have existed since the [Warring States period](/wiki/Warring_States_period "Warring States period") and were set up nationwide by the [Qin dynasty](/wiki/Qin_dynasty "Qin dynasty").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) The number of counties in [China proper](/wiki/China_proper "China proper") gradually increased from dynasty to dynasty. As [Qin Shi Huang](/wiki/Qin_Shi_Huang "Qin Shi Huang") reorganized the counties after his unification, there were about 1,000. Under the Eastern [Han dynasty](/wiki/Han_dynasty "Han dynasty"), the number of counties increased to above 1,000. About 1400 existed when the [Sui dynasty](/wiki/Sui_dynasty "Sui dynasty") abolished the [commandery](/wiki/Commandery_(China) "Commandery (China)") level (郡 jùn), which was the level just above counties, and demoted some commanderies to counties.

In Imperial China, the county was a significant administrative unit because it marked the lowest level of the imperial bureaucratic structure;[[6]](#cite_note-6) in other words, it was the lowest level that the government reached. Government below the county level was often undertaken through informal non-bureaucratic means, varying between dynasties. The head of a county was the [magistrate](/wiki/County_magistrate "County magistrate"), who oversaw both the day-to-day operations of the county as well as civil and criminal cases.

The current number of counties mostly resembled that of the later years of the [Qing dynasty](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty"). Changes of location and names of counties in [Chinese history](/wiki/History_of_China "History of China") have been a major field of research in Chinese historical geography, especially from the 1960s to the 1980s. There are 1,355 counties in [mainland China](/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China") out of a total of 2,851 county-level divisions.

## Taiwan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Taiwan")]

Main article: [County (Taiwan)](/wiki/County_(Taiwan) "County (Taiwan)")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/County_seat "Special:EditPage/County seat") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(August 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c9/%E8%8B%97%E6%A0%97%E7%B8%A3%E6%94%BF%E5%BA%9C_Miaoli_County_Government_-_panoramio.jpg/250px-%E8%8B%97%E6%A0%97%E7%B8%A3%E6%94%BF%E5%BA%9C_Miaoli_County_Government_-_panoramio.jpg)](/wiki/File:%E8%8B%97%E6%A0%97%E7%B8%A3%E6%94%BF%E5%BA%9C_Miaoli_County_Government_-_panoramio.jpg)

[Miaoli](/wiki/Miaoli "Miaoli"), the county seat of [Miaoli County](/wiki/Miaoli_County "Miaoli County") in Taiwan

In [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), the first counties were first established in 1661 by the [Kingdom of Tungning](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Tungning "Kingdom of Tungning"). The later ruler [Qing empire](/wiki/Taiwan_under_Qing_rule "Taiwan under Qing rule") inherited this type of administrative divisions. With the increase of [Han Chinese](/wiki/Han_Chinese "Han Chinese") population in Taiwan, the number of counties also grew by time. By the end of Qing era, there were 11 counties in Taiwan. [Protestant missionaries](/wiki/Protestant_missions_in_China "Protestant missions in China") in China first romanized the term as **hien**.[[7]](#cite_note-7) When [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan_under_Japanese_rule "Taiwan under Japanese rule") became a [Japanese colony](/wiki/Empire_of_Japan "Empire of Japan") in 1895, the hierarchy of divisions also incorporated into the [Japanese system](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Japan "Administrative divisions of Japan") in the period when Taiwan was under Japanese rule.

By September 1945, Taiwan was divided into 8 [prefectures](/wiki/Prefectures_of_Japan "Prefectures of Japan") (州 and 廳), which remained after the [Republic of China](/wiki/Republic_of_China_(1912%E2%80%931949) "Republic of China (1912–1949)") took over.

There are 13 county seats in Taiwan, which function as [county-administered cities](/wiki/County-administered_city "County-administered city"), [urban townships](/wiki/Township_(Taiwan) "Township (Taiwan)"), or [rural townships](/wiki/Township_(Taiwan) "Township (Taiwan)").

## United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: United States")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/Allentown.jpg/250px-Allentown.jpg)](/wiki/File:Allentown.jpg)

[Allentown, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Allentown,_Pennsylvania "Allentown, Pennsylvania"), the third-largest city in [Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pennsylvania "Pennsylvania") and county seat of [Lehigh County](/wiki/Lehigh_County,_Pennsylvania "Lehigh County, Pennsylvania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/Renville_County_Courthouse_MN.jpg/250px-Renville_County_Courthouse_MN.jpg)](/wiki/File:Renville_County_Courthouse_MN.jpg)

Many county seats in the United States feature a historic courthouse, such as this one in [Renville County, Minnesota](/wiki/Renville_County,_Minnesota "Renville County, Minnesota").

### Function

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Function")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/QACourthouse_QueenstownMD.jpg/250px-QACourthouse_QueenstownMD.jpg)](/wiki/File:QACourthouse_QueenstownMD.jpg)

The old courthouse in [Centreville, Maryland](/wiki/Centreville,_Maryland "Centreville, Maryland"), the county seat of [Queen Anne's County, Maryland](/wiki/Queen_Anne%27s_County,_Maryland "Queen Anne's County, Maryland"), U.S.

In most of the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), a [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") is an [administrative](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division") or political subdivision of a [state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") that consists of a geographic area with specific [boundaries](/wiki/Border "Border") and usually some level of governmental authority.[[8]](#cite_note-NACO-8) The city, town, or populated place that houses county government is known as the **seat** of its county. Generally, the county legislature, [county courthouse](/wiki/County_courthouse "County courthouse"), [sheriff's department headquarters](/wiki/Sheriffs_in_the_United_States "Sheriffs in the United States"), hall of records, [jail](/wiki/County_Jail "County Jail") and [correctional facility](/wiki/Correctional_facility "Correctional facility") are located in the county seat, though some functions (such as highway maintenance, which usually requires a large garage for vehicles, along with asphalt and salt storage facilities) may also be located or conducted in other parts of the county, especially if it is geographically large.

A county seat is usually an incorporated [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality"). The exceptions include the county seats of counties that have no incorporated municipalities within their borders, such as [Arlington County, Virginia](/wiki/Arlington_County,_Virginia "Arlington County, Virginia"), where the county seat is the entire county.[[9]](#cite_note-9) [Ellicott City](/wiki/Ellicott_City,_Maryland "Ellicott City, Maryland"), the county seat of [Howard County, Maryland](/wiki/Howard_County,_Maryland "Howard County, Maryland"), is the largest unincorporated county seat in the United States, followed by [Towson](/wiki/Towson,_Maryland "Towson, Maryland"), the county seat of [Baltimore County, Maryland](/wiki/Baltimore_County,_Maryland "Baltimore County, Maryland"). Likewise, some county seats may not be incorporated in their own right, but are located within incorporated municipalities. For example, [Cape May Court House, New Jersey](/wiki/Cape_May_Court_House,_New_Jersey "Cape May Court House, New Jersey"), though unincorporated, is a section of [Middle Township](/wiki/Middle_Township,_New_Jersey "Middle Township, New Jersey"), an incorporated municipality. In some states, often those that were among the original [Thirteen Colonies](/wiki/Thirteen_Colonies "Thirteen Colonies"), county seats include or formerly included "Court House" as part of their name, such as [Spotsylvania Courthouse, Virginia](/wiki/Spotsylvania_Courthouse,_Virginia "Spotsylvania Courthouse, Virginia").

### U.S. counties with more than one county seat

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: U.S. counties with more than one county seat")]

Most counties have only one county seat. However, some counties in [Alabama](/wiki/Alabama "Alabama"), [Arkansas](/wiki/Arkansas "Arkansas"), [California](/wiki/California "California") [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*], [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(U.S._state) "Georgia (U.S. state)"), [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa"), [Kentucky](/wiki/Kentucky "Kentucky"), [Massachusetts](/wiki/Massachusetts "Massachusetts"), [Mississippi](/wiki/Mississippi "Mississippi"), [Missouri](/wiki/Missouri "Missouri"), [New Hampshire](/wiki/New_Hampshire "New Hampshire"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), and [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont") have two or more county seats, usually located on opposite sides of the county. Examples include [Harrison County, Mississippi](/wiki/Harrison_County,_Mississippi "Harrison County, Mississippi"), which has both [Biloxi](/wiki/Biloxi,_Mississippi "Biloxi, Mississippi") and [Gulfport](/wiki/Gulfport,_Mississippi "Gulfport, Mississippi") as county seats, and [Hinds County, Mississippi](/wiki/Hinds_County,_Mississippi "Hinds County, Mississippi"), which has both [Raymond](/wiki/Raymond,_Mississippi "Raymond, Mississippi") and the state capital of [Jackson](/wiki/Jackson,_Mississippi "Jackson, Mississippi"). The practice of multiple county seat towns dates from the days when travel was difficult. There have been few efforts to eliminate the two-seat arrangement, since a county seat is a source of [civic pride](/wiki/Boosterism "Boosterism") for the towns involved, along with providing employment opportunities. By virtue of the unique term endemic only to its state, [Bennington County, Vermont](/wiki/Bennington_County,_Vermont "Bennington County, Vermont") is US only county to have two "shire towns", being [Manchester, Vermont](/wiki/Manchester,_Vermont "Manchester, Vermont") as the North Shire and the eponymously named [Bennington](/wiki/Bennington "Bennington") as the South Shire; the rugged topography in the rural county necessitates two accessible seats of government.

There are 33 counties with multiple county seats in 11 states:

* [Coffee County, Alabama](/wiki/Coffee_County,_Alabama "Coffee County, Alabama")[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* [St. Clair County, Alabama](/wiki/St._Clair_County,_Alabama "St. Clair County, Alabama")
* [Arkansas County, Arkansas](/wiki/Arkansas_County,_Arkansas "Arkansas County, Arkansas")

* [Bennington County, Vermont](/wiki/Bennington_County,_Vermont "Bennington County, Vermont")

### Other variations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Other variations")]

#### Alaska

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Alaska")]

[Alaska](/wiki/Alaska "Alaska") is divided into [boroughs](/wiki/List_of_boroughs_and_census_areas_in_Alaska "List of boroughs and census areas in Alaska") rather than counties; the county seat in these case is referred to as the "borough seat"; this includes six consolidated city-borough governments (one of which is styled as a "municipality"). The [Unorganized Borough, Alaska](/wiki/Unorganized_Borough,_Alaska "Unorganized Borough, Alaska"), which covers 49% of the state's area, has no borough government or borough seat. One borough, the [Lake and Peninsula Borough](/wiki/Lake_and_Peninsula_Borough,_Alaska "Lake and Peninsula Borough, Alaska"), has its borough seat located in another borough, namely [King Salmon](/wiki/King_Salmon,_Alaska "King Salmon, Alaska") in [Bristol Bay Borough](/wiki/Bristol_Bay_Borough,_Alaska "Bristol Bay Borough, Alaska").

#### Louisiana

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Louisiana")]

In [Louisiana](/wiki/Louisiana "Louisiana"), which is divided into [parishes](/wiki/List_of_parishes_in_Louisiana "List of parishes in Louisiana") rather than counties, county seats are referred to as "parish seats".

#### New England

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: New England")]

In [New England](/wiki/New_England "New England"), counties have served mainly as dividing lines for the states' judicial systems. [Rhode Island](/wiki/Rhode_Island "Rhode Island") has no county level of government and thus no county seats, and [Massachusetts](/wiki/Massachusetts "Massachusetts") has dissolved many but not all of its county governments. In [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont"), [Massachusetts](/wiki/Massachusetts "Massachusetts"),[[11]](#cite_note-11) and [Maine](/wiki/Maine "Maine")[[12]](#cite_note-12) county government consists only of a [Superior Court](/wiki/Superior_Court "Superior Court") and [Sheriff](/wiki/Sheriffs_in_the_United_States "Sheriffs in the United States") (as an [officer of the court](/wiki/Officer_of_the_court "Officer of the court")), both located in a designated "shire town". Bennington County, Vermont has two shire towns; the court for "North Shire" is in the shire town [Manchester](/wiki/Manchester,_Vermont "Manchester, Vermont"), and the Sheriff for the county and court for "South Shire" are in the shire town Bennington.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In 2024, [Connecticut](/wiki/Connecticut "Connecticut"), which had not defined their counties for anything but statistical, historical and weather warning purposes since 1960, along with ending the use of county seats in particular, will fully transition with the permission of the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau") to a system of [councils of government](/wiki/Councils_of_governments_in_Connecticut "Councils of governments in Connecticut") for the purposes of boundary definition and as county equivalents.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

#### South Dakota

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: South Dakota")]

Two counties in [South Dakota](/wiki/South_Dakota "South Dakota"), [Oglala Lakota](/wiki/Oglala_Lakota_County,_South_Dakota "Oglala Lakota County, South Dakota") and [Todd](/wiki/Todd_County,_South_Dakota "Todd County, South Dakota"), have their county seat and government services centered in a neighboring county. Their county-level services are provided by [Fall River County](/wiki/Fall_River_County,_South_Dakota "Fall River County, South Dakota") and [Tripp County](/wiki/Tripp_County,_South_Dakota "Tripp County, South Dakota"), respectively.[[16]](#cite_note-Sdcounties-16)

#### Virginia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=County_seat&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Virginia")]

In [Virginia](/wiki/Virginia "Virginia"), a county seat may be an [independent city](/wiki/Independent_city_(United_States) "Independent city (United States)") surrounded by, but not part of, the county of which it is the administrative center; for example, [Fairfax City](/wiki/Fairfax,_Virginia "Fairfax, Virginia") is both the county seat of [Fairfax County, Virginia](/wiki/Fairfax_County,_Virginia "Fairfax County, Virginia") and completely surrounded by Fairfax County, but the city is politically independent of the county. When the county seat is in the independent city, government offices such as the courthouse may be in the independent city under an agreement, such as in [Albemarle](/wiki/Albemarle_County,_Virginia "Albemarle County, Virginia"), or may in be [enclaves](/wiki/Enclave_and_exclave "Enclave and exclave") of the county surrounded by the independent city, such as in [Fairfax](/wiki/Fairfax_County,_Virginia "Fairfax County, Virginia"). Others, such as [Prince William](/wiki/Prince_William_County,_Virginia "Prince William County, Virginia"), have the courthouse in an enclave surrounded by the independent city and have the county government, the Board of Supervisors, in a different part of the county, far from the county seat. The following counties have their county seat in an independent city:

* [Albemarle County](/wiki/Albemarle_County,_Virginia "Albemarle County, Virginia") ([Charlottesville](/wiki/Charlottesville,_Virginia "Charlottesville, Virginia"))
* [Alleghany County](/wiki/Alleghany_County,_Virginia "Alleghany County, Virginia") ([Covington](/wiki/Covington,_Virginia "Covington, Virginia"))
* [Augusta County](/wiki/Augusta_County,_Virginia "Augusta County, Virginia") ([Staunton](/wiki/Staunton,_Virginia "Staunton, Virginia"))
* [Fairfax County](/wiki/Fairfax_County,_Virginia "Fairfax County, Virginia") ([Fairfax](/wiki/Fairfax,_Virginia "Fairfax, Virginia"))
* [Frederick County](/wiki/Frederick_County,_Virginia "Frederick County, Virginia") ([Winchester](/wiki/Winchester,_Virginia "Winchester, Virginia"))
* [Greensville County](/wiki/Greensville_County,_Virginia "Greensville County, Virginia") ([Emporia](/wiki/Emporia,_Virginia "Emporia, Virginia"))
* [Henry County](/wiki/Henry_County,_Virginia "Henry County, Virginia") ([Martinsville](/wiki/Martinsville,_Virginia "Martinsville, Virginia"))
* [James City County](/wiki/James_City_County,_Virginia "James City County, Virginia") ([Williamsburg](/wiki/Williamsburg,_Virginia "Williamsburg, Virginia"))
* [Prince William County](/wiki/Prince_William_County,_Virginia "Prince William County, Virginia") ([Manassas](/wiki/Manassas,_Virginia "Manassas, Virginia"))
* [Roanoke County](/wiki/Roanoke_County,_Virginia "Roanoke County, Virginia") ([Salem](/wiki/Salem,_Virginia "Salem, Virginia"))
* [Rockbridge County](/wiki/Rockbridge_County,_Virginia "Rockbridge County, Virginia") ([Lexington](/wiki/Lexington,_Virginia "Lexington, Virginia"))
* [Rockingham County](/wiki/Rockingham_County,_Virginia "Rockingham County, Virginia") ([Harrisonburg](/wiki/Harrisonburg,_Virginia "Harrisonburg, Virginia"))

[Bedford](/wiki/Bedford,_Virginia "Bedford, Virginia") was an independent city from 1968 to 2013, while also being the county seat of [Bedford County](/wiki/Bedford_County,_Virginia "Bedford County, Virginia"). Bedford reverted to an incorporated town, and remains the county seat, though is now part of the county.
